page: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 4345P processor featuring 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading, operating at a base frequency of 3.9 GHz. The system includes 123.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides approximately 7.71 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is comprised of a bundled 3890 GB NVMe SSD, and the server is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU scoring 4628.19 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 49.88 GB/sec. The PassMark memory score is also in the top 10% at 3278.15. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth show some tradeoffs. Multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier, while large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is weak at 21.85 GB/sec.

This bare metal configuration offers high resource density and dedicated physical hardware, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. The combination of high-capacity NVMe storage and top-tier single-core execution speeds makes this server suitable for database operations, web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks, though workloads heavily dependent on high-throughput multi-threaded memory writes may experience performance bottlenecks.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P processor with 16 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. It features 123.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory and a bundled 3890 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution metrics. However, large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25%. This dedicated hardware profile delivers high resource density without virtualization overhead, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy and single-thread-bound workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and data decompression tasks.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 4345P CPU, 123 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 3890 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and web workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining top-tier single-core performance with high-capacity NVMe storage and DDR5 memory for data-intensive workloads.
